Firm.title: Firm
Firm.description: Represents a single multi-tenant firm within the SaaS application, serving as the highest organizational unit.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Firm entity.

firmName.description: The legal or registered name of the firm.

subscriptionPlan.description: The current subscription plan the firm is enrolled in (e.g., 'Free', 'Standard', 'Premium').

subscriptionExpiry.description: The date and time when the firm's current subscription plan expires.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the firm's account is currently active and operational.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the firm record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the firm record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the firm record has been soft-deleted.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account with authentication details and roles within a firm or as a SuperAdmin.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm this user belongs to. (Relationship: Firm 1:N User). This is optional for 'SuperAdmin' role users.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login.

passwordHash.description: The hashed password for the user. Passwords are not stored directly, only their cryptographically secure hash.

role.description: The role of the user, determining access levels (e.g., 'SuperAdmin', 'FirmAdmin', 'Staff', 'ClientUser').

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the user account is currently active.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last successful login.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the user record has been soft-deleted.

Company.title: Company
Company.description: Represents a company managed by a firm within the accounting system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Company entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm that manages this company. (Relationship: Firm 1:N Company)

companyName.description: The legal or registered name of the company.

gstin.description: Goods and Services Tax Identification Number (GSTIN) of the company.

address.description: The registered address of the company.

financialYearStart.description: The start date of the company's financial year.

financialYearEnd.description: The end date of the company's financial year.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the company is currently active.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the company record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the company record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the company record has been soft-deleted.

Ledger.title: Ledger
Ledger.description: Represents an accounting ledger, used to record financial transactions and maintain balances.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ledger entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm this ledger belongs to. (Relationship: Firm 1:N Ledger)

companyId.description: Reference to the Company this ledger belongs to. (Relationship: Company 1:N Ledger)

ledgerName.description: The name of the ledger (e.g., 'Cash Account', 'Sales Account', 'Creditors').

group.description: The accounting group the ledger belongs to (e.g., 'Assets', 'Liabilities', 'Income', 'Expense').

openingBalance.description: The initial balance of the ledger at the start of a financial period.

balanceType.description: The natural balance type of the ledger (e.g., 'Dr' for Debit, 'Cr' for Credit).

gstApplicable.description: Indicates whether GST is applicable to transactions posted to this ledger.

gstRate.description: The default GST rate applicable to this ledger, if gstApplicable is true. (e.g., 5, 12, 18, 28).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the ledger record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the ledger record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the ledger record has been soft-deleted.

Voucher.title: Voucher
Voucher.description: Represents an accounting voucher, recording a financial transaction with detailed line items and double-entry accounting principles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Voucher entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm this voucher belongs to. (Relationship: Firm 1:N Voucher)

companyId.description: Reference to the Company this voucher belongs to. (Relationship: Company 1:N Voucher)

voucherNumber.description: A unique, auto-sequenced number for the voucher within a company.

voucherType.description: The type of the accounting voucher (e.g., 'Sales', 'Purchase', 'Payment', 'Receipt', 'Journal', 'Contra').

date.description: The date of the financial transaction.

partyLedgerId.description: Reference to the main party ledger involved in the transaction (e.g., customer, vendor, bank, cash). (Relationship: Ledger 1:N Voucher)

lineItems.description: An array of individual ledger entries (debit/credit) that make up the voucher, ensuring double-entry accounting.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the voucher, summing up all line items. This should balance to zero across debits and credits for a journal entry.

createdById.description: Reference to the User who created this voucher. (Relationship: User 1:N Voucher)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the voucher record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the voucher record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the voucher record has been soft-deleted.

VoucherLineItem.title: VoucherLineItem
VoucherLineItem.description: Represents an individual debit or credit entry within a Voucher, detailing the ledger and amount.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VoucherLineItem entity.

voucherId.description: Reference to the Voucher this line item belongs to. (Relationship: Voucher 1:N VoucherLineItem)

ledgerId.description: Reference to the Ledger affected by this line item. (Relationship: Ledger 1:N VoucherLineItem)

amount.description: The amount for this ledger entry. Positive for debit, negative for credit (or separate debit/credit fields).

taxRate.description: The tax rate applicable to this line item, if any.

taxAmount.description: The calculated tax amount for this line item.

isDebit.description: Indicates if this line item is a Debit (true) or Credit (false).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the voucher line item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the voucher line item record was last updated.

InvoiceTemplate.title: InvoiceTemplate
InvoiceTemplate.description: Defines a customizable template for generating invoices, specific to a company.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvoiceTemplate entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm this invoice template belongs to. (Relationship: Firm 1:N InvoiceTemplate)

companyId.description: Reference to the Company this invoice template belongs to. (Relationship: Company 1:N InvoiceTemplate)

templateName.description: A descriptive name for the invoice template.

logoUrl.description: URL to the company's logo to be used on the invoice.

colorTheme.description: JSON string or hex code defining the color theme for the invoice template (e.g., primary, accent colors).

bankDetails.description: JSON string or plain text containing bank account details for payments to appear on the invoice.

footerNotes.description: Customizable footer notes to appear on the invoice.

customFieldsSchemaId.description: Reference to the CustomFieldSchema defining dynamic custom fields for this template. (Relationship: CustomFieldSchema 1:N InvoiceTemplate)

isActive.description: Indicates whether this invoice template is currently active and usable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice template record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice template record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the invoice template record has been soft-deleted.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents a generated GST-compliant invoice document, usually linked to a Sales Voucher.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm this invoice belongs to. (Relationship: Firm 1:N Invoice)

companyId.description: Reference to the Company this invoice belongs to. (Relationship: Company 1:N Invoice)

voucherId.description: Reference to the Sales Voucher from which this invoice was generated. (Relationship: Voucher 1:N Invoice)

invoiceDate.description: The date the invoice was issued.

templateId.description: Reference to the InvoiceTemplate used to generate this invoice. (Relationship: InvoiceTemplate 1:N Invoice)

invoiceData.description: A JSON string containing the complete, structured data of the generated invoice at the time of creation (e.g., party details, line items, totals, tax breakdown).

pdfUrl.description: URL to the generated PDF version of the invoice.

status.description: The current status of the invoice (e.g., 'Draft', 'Generated', 'Sent', 'Paid', 'Cancelled', 'Overdue').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the invoice record has been soft-deleted.

CustomFieldSchema.title: CustomFieldSchema
CustomFieldSchema.description: Stores JSON schema definitions for custom fields that can be dynamically applied to various entities per company.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CustomFieldSchema entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm this custom field schema belongs to. (Relationship: Firm 1:N CustomFieldSchema)

companyId.description: Reference to the Company this custom field schema belongs to. (Relationship: Company 1:N CustomFieldSchema)

entityType.description: The type of entity to which these custom fields apply (e.g., 'Ledger', 'Invoice', 'Company').

schemaName.description: A descriptive name for this specific custom field schema (e.g., 'Invoice Custom Fields for Services').

schemaDefinition.description: A JSON string containing the actual JSON Schema defining the custom fields, their types, and validation rules.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the custom field schema record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the custom field schema record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the custom field schema record has been soft-deleted.

BankAccount.title: BankAccount
BankAccount.description: Represents a bank account associated with a company, used for bank uploads and reconciliation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BankAccount entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm this bank account belongs to. (Relationship: Firm 1:N BankAccount)

companyId.description: Reference to the Company this bank account belongs to. (Relationship: Company 1:N BankAccount)

accountName.description: A descriptive name for the bank account (e.g., 'Main Current Account').

ifscCode.description: The Indian Financial System Code (IFSC) of the bank branch.

branchName.description: The name of the bank branch where the account is held.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the bank account record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the bank account record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the bank account record has been soft-deleted.

BankTransaction.title: BankTransaction
BankTransaction.description: Represents a transaction imported from a bank statement, pending reconciliation with internal vouchers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BankTransaction entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm this bank transaction belongs to. (Relationship: Firm 1:N BankTransaction)

companyId.description: Reference to the Company this bank transaction belongs to. (Relationship: Company 1:N BankTransaction)

bankAccountId.description: Reference to the BankAccount this transaction was imported from. (Relationship: BankAccount 1:N BankTransaction)

transactionDate.description: The date the bank transaction occurred.

description.description: A description of the transaction from the bank statement.

referenceNumber.description: A reference number for the transaction (e.g., cheque number, UPI transaction ID).

status.description: The current status of the bank transaction (e.g., 'Imported', 'Matched', 'Pending', 'Ignored').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the bank transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the bank transaction record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the bank transaction record has been soft-deleted.

TallyImportLog.title: TallyImportLog
TallyImportLog.description: Records details of Tally XML imports, including status and any errors encountered during processing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TallyImportLog entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm this import log belongs to. (Relationship: Firm 1:N TallyImportLog)

companyId.description: Reference to the Company this import log belongs to. (Relationship: Company 1:N TallyImportLog)

importDate.description: The date and time when the Tally XML file was imported.

fileName.description: The name of the original Tally XML file uploaded.

status.description: The processing status of the import (e.g., 'Uploaded', 'Processing', 'Completed', 'Failed').

uploadedById.description: Reference to the User who initiated the Tally import. (Relationship: User 1:N TallyImportLog)

errorMessage.description: Detailed error message if the import failed or encountered issues.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the Tally import log record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the Tally import log record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the Tally import log record has been soft-deleted.

TallyMapping.title: TallyMapping
TallyMapping.description: Stores rules for mapping Tally entities (e.g., ledgers, voucher types) to internal system entities for automated import.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TallyMapping entity.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm this mapping belongs to. (Relationship: Firm 1:N TallyMapping)

companyId.description: Reference to the Company this mapping belongs to. (Relationship: Company 1:N TallyMapping)

mappingType.description: The type of entity being mapped (e.g., 'Ledger', 'VoucherType').

sourceIdentifier.description: The identifier from the Tally XML (e.g., Tally Ledger Name, Tally Voucher Type Name) to be mapped.

targetLedgerId.description: Reference to the internal Ledger entity if mappingType is 'Ledger'. (Relationship: Ledger 1:N TallyMapping)

targetVoucherType.description: The internal Voucher Type string (e.g., 'Sales', 'Purchase') if mappingType is 'VoucherType'.

targetEntityName.description: A descriptive name of the internal target entity (e.g., 'Cash Ledger', 'Sales Voucher Type') for display purposes.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the Tally mapping record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the Tally mapping record was last updated.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the Tally mapping record has been soft-deleted.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Records every significant user or system activity for auditing, security, and compliance purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditLog entry.

firmId.description: Reference to the Firm where the activity occurred. (Relationship: Firm 1:N AuditLog)

companyId.description: Reference to the Company where the activity occurred, if applicable. (Relationship: Company 1:N AuditLog)

timestamp.description: The date and time when the activity was recorded.

userId.description: Reference to the User who performed the action. (Relationship: User 1:N AuditLog)

entityType.description: The type of entity that was affected by the action (e.g., 'Firm', 'Company', 'Ledger', 'Voucher', 'User').

entityId.description: The unique identifier of the entity that was affected by the action.

oldValues.description: A JSON string representing the state of the entity's changed fields before modification, for auditing changes.

newValues.description: A JSON string representing the state of the entity's changed fields after modification, for auditing changes.

ipAddress.description: The IP address from which the action was initiated.

userAgent.description: The user agent string of the client that performed the action.

definition.description: Top-level collection for each multi-tenant firm. Accessible by 'SuperAdmin' and its 'FirmAdmin'. Includes 'firmId' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Firm.

definition.description: Stores user profiles, including 'firmId' and 'role' for role-based access control. 'request.auth.uid' must match '{userId}' for profile access. 'firmId' and 'role' are critical for rules-based access to firm-specific data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for 'SuperAdmin' role authorization. A document exists here if the 'userId' is a 'SuperAdmin'. Used for simple 'exists()' checks in security rules to grant global access without relying on 'get()' to the 'users' collection for role details.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the SuperAdmin User.

definition.description: Stores company details belonging to a specific firm. Includes denormalized 'firmId' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Company.

definition.description: Manages accounting ledgers for a specific company within a firm. Includes denormalized 'firmId' and 'companyId' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Company.

params.name: ledgerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Ledger.

definition.description: Stores accounting vouchers for a specific company within a firm, adhering to double-entry principles. Includes denormalized 'firmId', 'companyId', and 'createdById' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Company.

params.name: voucherId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Voucher.

definition.description: Individual debit/credit entries for a voucher. CRITICAL: To enable Authorization Independence, each VoucherLineItem document MUST include denormalized 'firmId' and 'companyId' fields, which are not currently in its schema. This avoids needing to get() the parent Voucher document for authorization checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Company.

params.name: voucherId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Voucher.

params.name: voucherLineItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the VoucherLineItem.

definition.description: Customizable invoice templates for a specific company within a firm. Includes denormalized 'firmId' and 'companyId' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Company.

params.name: invoiceTemplateId
params.description: The unique identifier of the InvoiceTemplate.

definition.description: Generated GST-compliant invoices linked to sales vouchers for a specific company within a firm. Includes denormalized 'firmId' and 'companyId' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Company.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Invoice.

definition.description: Stores JSON schema definitions for custom fields applicable to various entities within a specific company. Includes denormalized 'firmId' and 'companyId' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Company.

params.name: customFieldSchemaId
params.description: The unique identifier of the CustomFieldSchema.

definition.description: Bank accounts associated with a specific company within a firm. Includes denormalized 'firmId' and 'companyId' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Company.

params.name: bankAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the BankAccount.

definition.description: Transactions imported from bank statements for reconciliation. Includes denormalized 'firmId', 'companyId', and 'bankAccountId' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Company.

params.name: bankAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent BankAccount.

params.name: bankTransactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the BankTransaction.

definition.description: Records logs of Tally XML imports for a specific company. Includes denormalized 'firmId', 'companyId', and 'uploadedById' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Company.

params.name: tallyImportLogId
params.description: The unique identifier of the TallyImportLog.

definition.description: Stores mapping rules for Tally entities to internal entities for automated import for a specific company. Includes denormalized 'firmId' and 'companyId' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Company.

params.name: tallyMappingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the TallyMapping.

definition.description: Records significant user and system activities at the firm level, with optional 'companyId'. Includes denormalized 'firmId', 'companyId' (if present), and 'userId' for direct access checks.

params.name: firmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Firm.

params.name: auditLogId
params.description: The unique identifier of the AuditLog.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed with a strong emphasis on multi-tenancy, authorization independence, and robust security rules, adhering strictly to the provided CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES. The architecture centers around a hierarchical path structure that mirrors the organizational hierarchy: `Firm` -> `Company` -> `Entity`. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Strategy A):**

1.  **Core Multi-Tenancy Identifiers:** Every document that belongs to a specific firm includes a `firmId` field. Documents belonging to a specific company within a firm additionally include a `companyId` field. This denormalization is crucial. It ensures that any security rule can verify the `firmId` and `companyId` of a document directly from `resource.data.firmId` and `resource.data.companyId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on a parent document (e.g., the parent `Firm` or `Company`). This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic writes (transactions/batches) and simplifying rule debugging.
2.  **User Profile for Roles:** The `/users/{userId}` collection stores each user's `firmId` and `role`. While Firestore rules do not support custom claims out of the box (as per DBAC mandate), the `request.auth.uid` can be used within rules to `get()` the user's corresponding document in `/users/{userId}` to retrieve their `firmId` and `role`. This is a controlled, single lookup to establish the user's context, rather than a chain of `get()` calls across data documents. To further support `SuperAdmin` role checks without a `get()` lookup for every rule, a separate `/roles_superAdmins/{userId}` collection is introduced. The mere existence of a document with `request.auth.uid` as its ID in this collection grants `SuperAdmin` privileges, allowing for an efficient `exists()` check.
3.  **Specific Denormalization:** The `VoucherLineItem` entity, as a subcollection, originally lacked `firmId` and `companyId` in its schema. For **critical authorization independence**, each `VoucherLineItem` document in `/firms/{firmId}/companies/{companyId}/vouchers/{voucherId}/voucherLineItems/{voucherLineItemId}` **MUST** include denormalized `firmId` and `companyId` fields. This ensures rules can check these identifiers directly on the line item document itself, avoiding a `get()` call to its parent `Voucher` document.

**Structural Segregation (Strategy B):**

The deep nesting of collections (`/firms/{firmId}/companies/{companyId}/...`) naturally segregates data. Each collection (e.g., `ledgers`, `vouchers`) holds documents of a homogeneous security posture: they all belong to a specific firm and company, and access is governed by the user's role within that firm/company. This simplifies security rules, as rules can be applied uniformly to all documents within a given path segment.

**Access Modeling (Strategy C) and QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

The chosen path-based ownership (`/firms/{firmId}/companies/{companyId}/{entityId}`) is highly effective for QAPs. When a user requests data from `/firms/FIRM_X/companies/COMPANY_Y/ledgers`, the query itself is implicitly filtered by `FIRM_X` and `COMPANY_Y`. Security rules can then simply verify that `request.auth.firmId` (obtained from the user's `/users/{userId}` document) matches `FIRM_X` and that the user's role permits access to `COMPANY_Y`'s data. This ensures that `list` operations are inherently secure and efficient, as the database's index on the path acts as the primary filter, and rules merely *authorize* the filtered result set. The denormalized `firmId` and `companyId` within each document further reinforce this by allowing direct `resource.data` checks within rules, preventing data leakage if a maliciously crafted query attempted to bypass path filters.

**Data Clarity and Predictability (Strategy D):**

*   **Explicit State Modeling:** Entities like `Invoice` and `BankTransaction` utilize a dedicated `status` field for clear state management. 
*   **Predictable Schema:** The JSON schemas define static keys, avoiding dynamic key structures. `Voucher.lineItems`, while an array of strings in its schema, is handled by making `VoucherLineItem` a subcollection, offering a clear structure. 
*   **Radical Consistency:** Standardized authorization fields (`firmId`, `companyId`, `createdById`, `userId`) and descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{firmId}`, `{companyId}`, `{voucherId}`) are used throughout the paths.

This architecture prioritizes security and debuggability by embedding authorization context directly into documents and leveraging Firestore's hierarchical nature, while also making a necessary exception for a single user profile lookup to establish the `request.auth.uid`'s role and firm context.
